mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rates above 8.5% signal deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing corporate earnings visibility and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high UNRATE regimes exceed 1.5% monthly volatility-adjusted returns, or t-test p-value > 0.1 for negative return association.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate weakening supply-side activity, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through reduced corporate investment demand.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in combined INDPRO/HOUST decline scenarios exceed 2.0% monthly volatility-adjusted returns, or interaction effect IC < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles characterized by rising federal funds rates and elevated inflation expectations generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through increased equity discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in tightening regimes exceed 3.0% monthly volatility-adjusted returns, or interaction effect IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth signals robust economic demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through increased corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in high GDPC1 growth periods fall below 1.0% monthly volatility-adjusted returns, or t-test p-value > 0.05 for positive association.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with low interest rates creates monetary policy uncertainty, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through increased market risk aversion.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in high CPI/low rate regimes exceed 1.0% monthly volatility-adjusted returns, or interaction effect IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll growth indicates a tightening labor market, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through elevated corporate profit margins.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in high PAYEMS growth periods fall below 0.5% monthly volatility-adjusted returns, or t-test p-value > 0.1 for positive association.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential construction activity signals strong demand for housing, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through increased consumer spending and corporate sector demand.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high HOUST regimes fall below 1.5% monthly volatility-adjusted returns, or t-test p-value > 0.05 for positive association.
